relationships.name: success
relationships.description: FlowFiles received from Kafka. Depending on demarcation strategy it is a flow file per message or a bundle of messages grouped by topic and partition.

bootstrap.servers.name: bootstrap.servers
bootstrap.servers.description: Comma-separated list of Kafka Brokers in the format host:port

topic.name: topic
topic.description: The name of the Kafka Topic(s) to pull from. More than one can be supplied if comma separated.

topic_type.name: topic_type
topic_type.description: Specifies whether the Topic(s) provided are a comma separated list of names or a single regular expression

allowableValue.description: Topic is a full topic name or comma separated list of names
Topic is a regex using the Java Pattern syntax

group.id.name: group.id
group.id.description: A Group ID is used to identify consumers that are within the same consumer group. Corresponds to Kafka's 'group.id' property.

Commit Offsets.name: Commit Offsets
Commit Offsets.description: Specifies whether or not this Processor should commit the offsets to Kafka after receiving messages. Typically, we want this value set to true so that messages that are received are not duplicated. However, in certain scenarios, we may want to avoid committing the offsets, that the data can be processed and later acknowledged by PublishKafkaRecord in order to provide Exactly Once semantics. See Processor's Usage / Additional Details for more information.

max-uncommit-offset-wait.name: max-uncommit-offset-wait
max-uncommit-offset-wait.description: Specifies the maximum amount of time allowed to pass before offsets must be committed. This value impacts how often offsets will be committed.  Committing offsets less often increases throughput but also increases the window of potential data duplication in the event of a rebalance or JVM restart between commits.  This value is also related to maximum poll records and the use of a message demarcator.  When using a message demarcator we can have far more uncommitted messages than when we're not as there is much less for us to keep track of in memory.

honor-transactions.name: honor-transactions
honor-transactions.description: Specifies whether or not NiFi should honor transactional guarantees when communicating with Kafka. If false, the Processor will use an "isolation level" of read_uncomitted. This means that messages will be received as soon as they are written to Kafka but will be pulled, even if the producer cancels the transactions. If this value is true, NiFi will not receive any messages for which the producer's transaction was canceled, but this can result in some latency since the consumer must wait for the producer to finish its entire transaction instead of pulling as the messages become available.

message-demarcator.name: message-demarcator
message-demarcator.description: Since KafkaConsumer receives messages in batches, you have an option to output FlowFiles which contains all Kafka messages in a single batch for a given topic and partition and this property allows you to provide a string (interpreted as UTF-8) to use for demarcating apart multiple Kafka messages. This is an optional property and if not provided each Kafka message received will result in a single FlowFile which  time it is triggered. To enter special character such as 'new line' use CTRL+Enter or Shift+Enter depending on the OS

separate-by-key.name: separate-by-key
separate-by-key.description: If true, and the <Message Demarcator> property is set, two messages will only be added to the same FlowFile if both of the Kafka Messages have identical keys.

security.protocol.name: security.protocol
security.protocol.description: Security protocol used to communicate with brokers. Corresponds to Kafka Client security.protocol property

sasl.mechanism.name: sasl.mechanism
sasl.mechanism.description: SASL mechanism used for authentication. Corresponds to Kafka Client sasl.mechanism property

allowableValue.description: General Security Services API for Kerberos authentication
Plain username and password authentication
Salted Challenge Response Authentication Mechanism using SHA-512 with username and password
Salted Challenge Response Authentication Mechanism using SHA-256 with username and password

kerberos-credentials-service.name: kerberos-credentials-service
kerberos-credentials-service.description: Service supporting generalized credentials authentication with Kerberos

kerberos-user-service.name: kerberos-user-service
kerberos-user-service.description: Service supporting user authentication with Kerberos

sasl.kerberos.service.name.name: sasl.kerberos.service.name
sasl.kerberos.service.name.description: The service name that matches the primary name of the Kafka server configured in the broker JAAS configuration

sasl.kerberos.principal.name: sasl.kerberos.principal
sasl.kerberos.principal.description: Principal used for authentication with Kerberos

sasl.kerberos.keytab.name: sasl.kerberos.keytab
sasl.kerberos.keytab.description: Keytab credentials used for authentication with Kerberos

sasl.username.name: sasl.username
sasl.username.description: Username provided with configured password when using PLAIN or SCRAM SASL Mechanisms

sasl.password.name: sasl.password
sasl.password.description: Password provided with configured username when using PLAIN or SCRAM SASL Mechanisms

sasl.token.auth.name: sasl.token.auth
sasl.token.auth.description: Enables or disables Token authentication when using SCRAM SASL Mechanisms

aws.profile.name.name: aws.profile.name
aws.profile.name.description: The Amazon Web Services Profile to select when multiple profiles are available.

ssl.context.service.name: ssl.context.service
ssl.context.service.description: Service supporting SSL communication with Kafka brokers

key-attribute-encoding.name: key-attribute-encoding
key-attribute-encoding.description: FlowFiles that are emitted have an attribute named 'kafka.key'. This property dictates how the value of the attribute should be encoded.

allowableValue.description: The key is interpreted as a UTF-8 Encoded string.
The key is interpreted as arbitrary binary data and is encoded using hexadecimal characters with uppercase letters

allowableValue.displayName: Do Not Add Key as Attribute
allowableValue.description: The key will not be added as an Attribute

auto.offset.reset.name: auto.offset.reset
auto.offset.reset.description: Allows you to manage the condition when there is no initial offset in Kafka or if the current offset does not exist any more on the server (e.g. because that data has been deleted). Corresponds to Kafka's 'auto.offset.reset' property.

allowableValue.description: Automatically reset the offset to the earliest offset
Automatically reset the offset to the latest offset
Throw exception to the consumer if no previous offset is found for the consumer's group

message-header-encoding.name: message-header-encoding
message-header-encoding.description: Any message header that is found on a Kafka message will be added to the outbound FlowFile as an attribute. This property indicates the Character Encoding to use for deserializing the headers.

header-name-regex.name: header-name-regex
header-name-regex.description: A Regular Expression that is matched against all message headers. Any message header whose name matches the regex will be added to the FlowFile as an Attribute. If not specified, no Header values will be added as FlowFile attributes. If two messages have a different value for the same header and that header is selected by the provided regex, then those two messages must be added to different FlowFiles. As a result, users should be cautious about using a regex like ".*" if messages are expected to have header values that are unique per message, such as an identifier or timestamp, because it will prevent NiFi from bundling the messages together efficiently.

max.poll.records.name: max.poll.records
max.poll.records.description: Specifies the maximum number of records Kafka should return in a single poll.

Communications Timeout.name: Communications Timeout
Communications Timeout.description: Specifies the timeout that the consumer should use when communicating with the Kafka Broker

relationships.name: failure
relationships.description: Any FlowFile that cannot be sent to Kafka will be routed to this Relationship

relationships.name: success
relationships.description: FlowFiles for which all content was sent to Kafka.

bootstrap.servers.name: bootstrap.servers
bootstrap.servers.description: Comma-separated list of Kafka Brokers in the format host:port

topic.name: topic
topic.description: The name of the Kafka Topic to publish to.

use-transactions.name: use-transactions
use-transactions.description: Specifies whether or not NiFi should provide Transactional guarantees when communicating with Kafka. If there is a problem sending data to Kafka, and this property is set to false, then the messages that have already been sent to Kafka will continue on and be delivered to consumers. If this is set to true, then the Kafka transaction will be rolled back so that those messages are not available to consumers. Setting this to true requires that the <Delivery Guarantee> property be set to "Guarantee Replicated Delivery."

transactional-id-prefix.name: transactional-id-prefix
transactional-id-prefix.description: When Use Transaction is set to true, KafkaProducer config 'transactional.id' will be a generated UUID and will be prefixed with this string.

message-demarcator.name: message-demarcator
message-demarcator.description: Specifies the string (interpreted as UTF-8) to use for demarcating multiple messages within a single FlowFile. If not specified, the entire content of the FlowFile will be used as a single message. If specified, the contents of the FlowFile will be split on this delimiter and each section sent as a separate Kafka message. To enter special character such as 'new line' use CTRL+Enter or Shift+Enter, depending on your OS.

Failure Strategy.name: Failure Strategy
Failure Strategy.description: Specifies how the processor handles a FlowFile if it is unable to publish the data to Kafka

allowableValue.description: When unable to publish records to Kafka, the FlowFile will be routed to the failure relationship.

When unable to publish records to Kafka, the FlowFile will be placed back on the queue so that it will be retried. For flows where FlowFile ordering is important, this strategy can be used along with ensuring that the each processor uses only a single Concurrent Task.

acks.name: acks
acks.description: Specifies the requirement for guaranteeing that a message is sent to Kafka. Corresponds to Kafka's 'acks' property.

allowableValue.description: FlowFile will be routed to success after successfully sending the content to a Kafka node, without waiting for any acknowledgment from the node at all. This provides the best performance but may result in data loss.

FlowFile will be routed to success if the message is received by a single Kafka node, whether or not it is replicated. This is faster than <Guarantee Replicated Delivery> but can result in data loss if a Kafka node crashes
FlowFile will be routed to failure unless the message is replicated to the appropriate number of Kafka Nodes according to the Topic configuration

attribute-name-regex.name: attribute-name-regex
attribute-name-regex.description: A Regular Expression that is matched against all FlowFile attribute names. Any attribute whose name matches the regex will be added to the Kafka messages as a Header. If not specified, no FlowFile attributes will be added as headers.

message-header-encoding.name: message-header-encoding
message-header-encoding.description: For any attribute that is added as a message header, as configured via the <Attributes to Send as Headers> property, this property indicates the Character Encoding to use for serializing the headers.

security.protocol.name: security.protocol
security.protocol.description: Security protocol used to communicate with brokers. Corresponds to Kafka Client security.protocol property

sasl.mechanism.name: sasl.mechanism
sasl.mechanism.description: SASL mechanism used for authentication. Corresponds to Kafka Client sasl.mechanism property

allowableValue.description: General Security Services API for Kerberos authentication
Plain username and password authentication
Salted Challenge Response Authentication Mechanism using SHA-512 with username and password
Salted Challenge Response Authentication Mechanism using SHA-256 with username and password

kerberos-credentials-service.name: kerberos-credentials-service
kerberos-credentials-service.description: Service supporting generalized credentials authentication with Kerberos

kerberos-user-service.name: kerberos-user-service
kerberos-user-service.description: Service supporting user authentication with Kerberos

sasl.kerberos.service.name.name: sasl.kerberos.service.name
sasl.kerberos.service.name.description: The service name that matches the primary name of the Kafka server configured in the broker JAAS configuration

sasl.kerberos.principal.name: sasl.kerberos.principal
sasl.kerberos.principal.description: Principal used for authentication with Kerberos

sasl.kerberos.keytab.name: sasl.kerberos.keytab
sasl.kerberos.keytab.description: Keytab credentials used for authentication with Kerberos

sasl.username.name: sasl.username
sasl.username.description: Username provided with configured password when using PLAIN or SCRAM SASL Mechanisms

sasl.password.name: sasl.password
sasl.password.description: Password provided with configured username when using PLAIN or SCRAM SASL Mechanisms

aws.profile.name.name: aws.profile.name
aws.profile.name.description: The Amazon Web Services Profile to select when multiple profiles are available.

sasl.token.auth.name: sasl.token.auth
sasl.token.auth.description: Enables or disables Token authentication when using SCRAM SASL Mechanisms

ssl.context.service.name: ssl.context.service
ssl.context.service.description: Service supporting SSL communication with Kafka brokers

kafka-key.name: kafka-key
kafka-key.description: The Key to use for the Message. If not specified, the flow file attribute 'kafka.key' is used as the message key, if it is present.Beware that setting Kafka key and demarcating at the same time may potentially lead to many Kafka messages with the same key.Normally this is not a problem as Kafka does not enforce or assume message and key uniqueness. Still, setting the demarcator and Kafka key at the same time poses a risk of data loss on Kafka. During a topic compaction on Kafka, messages will be deduplicated based on this key.

key-attribute-encoding.name: key-attribute-encoding
key-attribute-encoding.description: FlowFiles that are emitted have an attribute named 'kafka.key'. This property dictates how the value of the attribute should be encoded.

allowableValue.description: The key is interpreted as a UTF-8 Encoded string.
The key is interpreted as arbitrary binary data that is encoded using hexadecimal characters with uppercase letters.

max.request.size.name: max.request.size
max.request.size.description: The maximum size of a request in bytes. Corresponds to Kafka's 'max.request.size' property and defaults to 1 MB (1048576).

ack.wait.time.name: ack.wait.time
ack.wait.time.description: After sending a message to Kafka, this indicates the amount of time that we are willing to wait for a response from Kafka. If Kafka does not acknowledge the message within this time period, the FlowFile will be routed to 'failure'.

max.block.ms.name: max.block.ms
max.block.ms.description: The amount of time publisher will wait to obtain metadata or wait for the buffer to flush during the 'send' call before failing the entire 'send' call. Corresponds to Kafka's 'max.block.ms' property

partitioner.class.name: partitioner.class
partitioner.class.description: Specifies which class to use to compute a partition id for a message. Corresponds to Kafka's 'partitioner.class' property.

allowableValue.description: Messages will be assigned partitions in a round-robin fashion, sending the first message to Partition 1, the next Partition to Partition 2, and so on, wrapping as necessary.
The default partitioning strategy will choose the sticky partition that changes when the batch is full (See KIP-480 for details about sticky partitioning).

Interprets the <Partition> property as Expression Language that will be evaluated against each FlowFile. This Expression will be evaluated once against the FlowFile, so all Records in a given FlowFile will go to the same partition.

partition.name: partition
partition.description: Specifies which Partition Records will go to.

compression.type.name: compression.type
compression.type.description: This parameter allows you to specify the compression codec for all data generated by this producer.

relationships.name: unmatched
relationships.description: FlowFiles that do not match any user-define expression will be routed here

Routing Strategy.name: Routing Strategy
Routing Strategy.description: Specifies how to determine which relationship to use when evaluating the Expression Language

allowableValue.description: A copy of the FlowFile will be routed to each relationship whose corresponding expression evaluates to 'true'
Requires that all user-defined expressions evaluate to 'true' for the FlowFile to be considered a match
Requires that at least one user-defined expression evaluate to 'true' for the FlowFile to be considered a match

relationships.name: failure
relationships.description: Any FlowFile that cannot be sent to Kafka will be routed to this Relationship

relationships.name: success
relationships.description: FlowFiles for which all content was sent to Kafka.

bootstrap.servers.description: Comma-separated list of Kafka Brokers in the format host:port

topic.description: The name of the Kafka Topic to publish to.

message-header-encoding.description: For any attribute that is added as a message header, as configured via the <Attributes to Send as Headers> property, this property indicates the Character Encoding to use for serializing the headers.

partition.name: partition
partition.description: Specifies which Partition Records will go to.

compression.type.name: compression.type
compression.type.description: This parameter allows you to specify the compression codec for all data generated by this producer.

relationships.name: Failure
relationships.description: Request FlowFiles transferred when receiving socket communication errors.

relationships.name: No Retry
relationships.description: Request FlowFiles transferred when receiving HTTP responses with a status code between 400 an 499.

relationships.name: Original
relationships.description: Request FlowFiles transferred when receiving HTTP responses with a status code between 200 and 299.

relationships.name: Response
relationships.description: Response FlowFiles transferred when receiving HTTP responses with a status code between 200 and 299.

relationships.name: Retry
relationships.description: Request FlowFiles transferred when receiving HTTP responses with a status code between 500 and 599.

HTTP Method.name: HTTP Method
HTTP Method.description: HTTP request method (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS). Arbitrary methods are also supported. Methods other than POST, PUT and PATCH will be sent without a message body.

Remote URL.name: Remote URL
Remote URL.description: HTTP remote URL including a scheme of http or https, as well as a hostname or IP address with optional port and path elements. Any encoding of the URL must be done by the user.

disable-http2.name: disable-http2
disable-http2.description: Disable negotiation of HTTP/2 protocol. HTTP/2 requires TLS. HTTP/1.1 protocol supported is required when HTTP/2 is disabled.

SSL Context Service.name: SSL Context Service
SSL Context Service.description: SSL Context Service provides trusted certificates and client certificates for TLS communication.

Connection Timeout.name: Connection Timeout
Connection Timeout.description: Maximum time to wait for initial socket connection to the HTTP URL.

Read Timeout.name: Read Timeout
Read Timeout.description: Maximum time to wait for receiving responses from a socket connection to the HTTP URL.

Socket Write Timeout.name: Socket Write Timeout
Socket Write Timeout.description: Maximum time to wait for write operations while sending requests from a socket connection to the HTTP URL.

idle-timeout.name: idle-timeout
idle-timeout.description: Maximum time to wait before closing idle connections to the HTTP URL.

max-idle-connections.name: max-idle-connections
max-idle-connections.description: Maximum number of idle connections to the HTTP URL.

proxy-configuration-service.name: proxy-configuration-service
proxy-configuration-service.description: Specifies the Proxy Configuration Controller Service to proxy network requests. If set, it supersedes proxy settings configured per component. Supported proxies: HTTP + AuthN, SOCKS In case of SOCKS, it is not guaranteed that the selected SOCKS Version will be used by the processor.

Proxy Host.name: Proxy Host
Proxy Host.description: Proxy Host and dependent properties are deprecated in favor of Proxy Configuration Service. Proxy Host can be configured using an IP address or DNS address.

Proxy Port.name: Proxy Port
Proxy Port.description: Proxy Port and dependent properties are deprecated in favor of Proxy Configuration Service. Port number for the configured Proxy Host address.

Proxy Type.name: Proxy Type
Proxy Type.description: Proxy Type and dependent properties are deprecated in favor of Proxy Configuration Service. Proxy protocol type is not used

invokehttp-proxy-user.name: invokehttp-proxy-user
invokehttp-proxy-user.description: Proxy Username and dependent properties are deprecated in favor of Proxy Configuration Service. Username to set when authenticating with a Proxy server.

invokehttp-proxy-password.name: invokehttp-proxy-password
invokehttp-proxy-password.description: Proxy Password and dependent properties are deprecated in favor of Proxy Configuration Service. Password to set when authenticating with a Proxy server.

oauth2-access-token-provider.name: oauth2-access-token-provider
oauth2-access-token-provider.description: Enables managed retrieval of OAuth2 Bearer Token applied to HTTP requests using the Authorization Header.

Basic Authentication Username.name: Basic Authentication Username
Basic Authentication Username.description: The username provided for authentication of HTTP requests. Encoded using Base64 for HTTP Basic Authentication as described in RFC 7617.

Basic Authentication Password.name: Basic Authentication Password
Basic Authentication Password.description: The password provided for authentication of HTTP requests. Encoded using Base64 for HTTP Basic Authentication as described in RFC 7617.

Digest Authentication.name: Digest Authentication
Digest Authentication.description: Enable Digest Authentication on HTTP requests with Username and Password credentials as described in RFC 7616.

Penalize on \"No Retry\".name: Penalize on "No Retry"
Penalize on \"No Retry\".description: Enable penalization of request FlowFiles when receiving HTTP response with a status code between 400 and 499.

send-message-body.name: send-message-body
send-message-body.description: Enable sending HTTP request body for PATCH, POST, or PUT methods.

form-body-form-name.name: form-body-form-name
form-body-form-name.description: Enable sending HTTP request body formatted using multipart/form-data and using the form name configured.

set-form-filename.name: set-form-filename
set-form-filename.description: Enable sending the FlowFile filename attribute as the filename parameter in the Content-Disposition Header for multipart/form-data HTTP requests.

Use Chunked Encoding.name: Use Chunked Encoding
Use Chunked Encoding.description: Enable sending HTTP requests with the Transfer-Encoding Header set to chunked, and disable sending the Content-Length Header. Transfer-Encoding applies to the body in HTTP/1.1 requests as described in RFC 7230 Section 3.3.1

Content-Encoding.name: Content-Encoding
Content-Encoding.description: HTTP Content-Encoding applied to request body during transmission. The receiving server must support the selected encoding to avoid request failures.

allowableValue.description: Content encoding not applied during transmission
Gzip content encoding and HTTP Content-Encoding header applied during transmission

Content-Type.name: Content-Type
Content-Type.description: HTTP Content-Type Header applied to when sending an HTTP request body for PATCH, POST, or PUT methods. The Content-Type defaults to application/octet-stream when not configured.

Include Date Header.name: Include Date Header
Include Date Header.description: Enable sending HTTP Date Header on HTTP requests as described in RFC 7231 Section 7.1.1.2.

Attributes to Send.name: Attributes to Send
Attributes to Send.description: Regular expression that defines which FlowFile attributes to send as HTTP headers in the request. If not defined, no attributes are sent as headers. Dynamic properties will be always be sent as headers. The dynamic property name will be the header key and the dynamic property value, interpreted as Expression Language, will be the header value. Attributes and their values are limited to ASCII characters due to the requirement of the HTTP protocol.

Useragent.name: Useragent
Useragent.description: HTTP User-Agent Header applied to requests. RFC 7231 Section 5.5.3 describes recommend formatting.

Put Response Body In Attribute.name: Put Response Body In Attribute
Put Response Body In Attribute.description: FlowFile attribute name used to write an HTTP response body for FlowFiles transferred to the Original relationship.

Max Length To Put In Attribute.name: Max Length To Put In Attribute
Max Length To Put In Attribute.description: Maximum size in bytes applied when writing an HTTP response body to a FlowFile attribute. Attributes exceeding the maximum will be truncated.

ignore-response-content.name: ignore-response-content
ignore-response-content.description: Disable writing HTTP response FlowFiles to Response relationship

use-etag.name: use-etag
use-etag.description: Enable HTTP response caching described in RFC 7234. Caching responses considers ETag and other headers.

etag-max-cache-size.name: etag-max-cache-size
etag-max-cache-size.description: Maximum size of HTTP response cache in bytes. Caching responses considers ETag and other headers.

cookie-strategy.name: cookie-strategy
cookie-strategy.description: Strategy for accepting and persisting HTTP cookies. Accepting cookies enables persistence across multiple requests.

Always Output Response.name: Always Output Response
Always Output Response.description: Enable generation and transfer of a FlowFile to the Response relationship regardless of HTTP response received.

flow-file-naming-strategy.name: flow-file-naming-strategy
flow-file-naming-strategy.description: Determines the strategy used for setting the filename attribute of FlowFiles transferred to the Response relationship.

allowableValue.description: FlowFile filename attribute will be a random value.
FlowFile filename attribute will be extracted from the remote URL path. The attribute may contain URL encoded characters. If the path doesn't exist, the attribute will be a random value.

Add Response Headers to Request.name: Add Response Headers to Request
Add Response Headers to Request.description: Enable adding HTTP response headers as attributes to FlowFiles transferred to the Original relationship.

Follow Redirects.name: Follow Redirects
Follow Redirects.description: Enable following HTTP redirects sent with HTTP 300 series responses as described in RFC 7231 Section 6.4.
